intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant "Helmiopsis rigida." However, I found information on the plant "Heliotropium rigidum". Here's a paragraph about it:

Heliotropium rigidum, also known as Helmiopsis rigida, is a plant species within the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is used in traditional medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe various health issues. The bioactive constituents of Heliotropium rigidum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Heliotropium rigidum is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
